question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.totalUnits(id) and reverts if totalUnits exceeds a threshold, under PRECONDITIONS where the market has high total units, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: market accumulates units -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads totalUnits -> above threshold -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable regardless of market size, causing scoped impact: DoS on take in large markets? Proof idea: fill market to high totalUnits; deploy totalUnits-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.continuousFee(id) and reverts if the fee is zero, under PRECONDITIONS where the market has zero continuous fee, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: market has zero continuousFee -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads continuousFee -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable regardless of the market's continuous fee setting, causing scoped impact: DoS on take in zero-fee markets? Proof idea: deploy fee-checking ratifier; assert take reverts in zero-fee market.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.tickSpacing(id) and reverts if tickSpacing is not 1, under PRECONDITIONS where the market has default tick spacing (not 1), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: market has tickSpacing > 1 -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads tickSpacing -> not 1 -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable at any valid tick spacing, causing scoped impact: DoS on take in markets with non-unit tick spacing? Proof idea: deploy tickSpacing-checking ratifier; assert take reverts when tickSpacing > 1.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.pendingFee(id, maker) and reverts if pendingFee is nonzero, under PRECONDITIONS where the maker has accumulated pending continuous fees, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: maker accumulates pendingFee -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads pendingFee -> nonzero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable regardless of the maker's pending fee state, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for makers with pending fees? Proof idea: accumulate maker pendingFee; deploy pendingFee-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.lastAccrual(id, maker) and reverts if lastAccrual is not equal to block.timestamp, under PRECONDITIONS where the maker's position has not been updated in the current block, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: time passes without maker interaction -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads lastAccrual -> not current block -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable without requiring the maker to have interacted in the current block, causing scoped impact: DoS on take unless maker updates position every block? Proof idea: deploy lastAccrual-checking ratifier; assert take reverts unless maker called updatePosition in same block.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.collateralBitmap(id, seller) and reverts if the bitmap is zero (no collateral activated), under PRECONDITIONS where the seller has no activated collateral, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: seller has no collateral -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads collateralBitmap -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable even for sellers with no collateral (e.g., buy offers where seller is the taker), causing scoped impact: DoS on take for sellers with no activated collateral? Proof idea: deploy bitmap-checking ratifier; assert take reverts when seller has no collateral.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.isAuthorized(maker, taker) and only passes if taker is authorized by maker, effectively creating a whitelist ratifier, under PRECONDITIONS where the maker authorizes the ratifier but does not authorize the taker, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: taker (not in maker's whitelist) calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads isAuthorized(maker, taker) -> false -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that any address should be able to take a public offer, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for all non-whitelisted takers? Proof idea: deploy whitelist ratifier; assert take reverts for non-authorized takers.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.debtOf(id, buyer) and reverts if buyer debt is zero (i.e., buyer is not already a borrower), under PRECONDITIONS where the buyer is a new lender with no existing debt, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: new lender calls take(buyOffer, ...) -> ratifier reads debtOf(id, lender) -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a new lender must be able to take a buy offer, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for new lenders? Proof idea: deploy debt-checking ratifier; assert take reverts for lenders with zero debt.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.lossFactor(id) and reverts if lossFactor equals type(uint128).max - 1 (near maxed out), under PRECONDITIONS where the market is near loss factor maximum, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: market approaches max lossFactor -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads lossFactor -> near max -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that take must succeed for any lossFactor < type(uint128).max (the protocol's own check), causing scoped impact: DoS on take in distressed markets? Proof idea: drive lossFactor near max; deploy lossFactor-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.marketState(id).continuousFeeCredit and reverts if it is nonzero, under PRECONDITIONS where the market has accumulated continuous fee credit, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: continuous fees accumulate -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads continuousFeeCredit -> nonzero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable regardless of accumulated continuous fee credit, causing scoped impact: DoS on take in markets with any continuous fee? Proof idea: accumulate continuousFeeCredit; deploy credit-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.consumed(maker, group) BEFORE the consumed update in take (since isRatified is called at line 356, before consumed is updated at lines 367-373) and reverts if consumed is already at maxUnits - 1, under PRECONDITIONS where the offer is nearly exhausted, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: offer is nearly consumed -> taker calls take(1 unit, ...) -> ratifier reads consumed -> maxUnits - 1 -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that the last unit of an offer must be fillable, causing scoped impact: DoS on the final fill of any offer? Proof idea: fill offer to maxUnits - 1; deploy consumed-checking ratifier; assert final take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.position(id, seller).debt and reverts if seller debt is zero (seller has no debt), under PRECONDITIONS where the seller is a lender with credit but no debt, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: seller has credit but no debt -> taker calls take(buyOffer, ...) -> ratifier reads position.debt -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a lender with credit must be able to have their buy offer filled, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for lenders with no debt? Proof idea: deploy debt-zero-checking ratifier; assert take reverts for lenders.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.isAuthorized(maker, ratifier) (the same check done at line 355) and reverts if it returns false, creating a redundant check that can be exploited if the authorization is revoked between line 355 and line 356, under PRECONDITIONS where the ratifier is authorized at line 355 but the authorization is revoked in the same transaction via reentrancy, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: take begins -> line 355 passes -> ratifier.isRatified reads isAuthorized -> still true -> passes -> no issue, but if a reentrant call revokes authorization between 355 and 356 via a different path, violating INVARIANT that authorization state must be consistent within a single take call, causing scoped impact: potential TOCTOU DoS if authorization can be revoked mid-take? Proof idea: attempt to revoke authorization from within isRatified via STATICCALL (should fail since STATICCALL prevents state changes); confirm no TOCTOU exists.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.marketState(id).tickSpacing and reverts if tickSpacing has been reduced (i.e., tickSpacing < DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING), under PRECONDITIONS where the tickSpacingSetter has reduced the market's tick spacing, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: tickSpacing is reduced -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads tickSpacing -> below default -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable after tick spacing changes, causing scoped impact: DoS on take after tick spacing reduction? Proof idea: reduce tickSpacing; deploy tickSpacing-change-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.settlementFeeCbps(id) and reverts if any breakpoint fee has been changed from the default, under PRECONDITIONS where the feeSetter has modified the market's settlement fees, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: feeSetter changes settlement fee -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads settlementFeeCbps -> changed from default -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable after fee changes, causing scoped impact: DoS on take after settlement fee modification? Proof idea: change settlement fee; deploy fee-change-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.position(id, maker).credit and reverts if maker credit is above a threshold, under PRECONDITIONS where the maker (lender) has accumulated large credit, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: maker accumulates credit -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads credit -> above threshold -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a maker with large credit must still be able to have their offers filled, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for high-credit makers? Proof idea: accumulate maker credit; deploy credit-threshold ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.position(id, seller).collateral[i] for all i in [0, 127] in a loop, consuming excessive gas, under PRECONDITIONS where the ratifier is authorized and the seller has many activated collaterals, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: seller activates 16 collaterals -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier loops over all 128 collateral slots -> OOG -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid take must complete within reasonable gas bounds, causing scoped impact: griefing DoS on take for sellers with many collaterals? Proof idea: deploy a collateral-looping ratifier; measure gas consumption with 16 activated collaterals.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.toMarket(id) (which reconstructs the full Market struct from storage) and performs expensive computation on the collateralParams array, under PRECONDITIONS where the market has 128 collateral params and the ratifier is authorized, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: market with 128 collaterals is created -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier calls toMarket(id) -> iterates all 128 collateral params -> OOG -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid take must complete within block gas limit, causing scoped impact: griefing DoS on take in large multi-collateral markets? Proof idea: create market with max collaterals; deploy toMarket-iterating ratifier; measure gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.isAuthorized(maker, address(0)) and reverts if it returns true (which it never should per the addressZeroCantAuthorize invariant), under PRECONDITIONS where the ratifier is authorized, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads isAuthorized(maker, address(0)) -> always false -> ratifier passes -> no DoS, but if the invariant is broken and address(0) is somehow authorized, ratifier reverts, violating INVARIANT that address(0) can never be authorized, causing scoped impact: DoS on take if the addressZeroCantAuthorize invariant is violated? Proof idea: invariant test asserting isAuthorized(any, address(0)) is always false after any sequence of calls.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.liquidationLocked(id, buyer) and reverts if the buyer is liquidation-locked, under PRECONDITIONS where the buyer is currently in a nested take callback (liquidation-locked), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: buyer is in a nested take callback -> outer take calls ratifier.isRatified -> ratifier reads liquidationLocked(id, buyer) -> true -> ratifier reverts -> outer take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid nested take must succeed if the buyer is not the seller, causing scoped impact: DoS on nested take flows where the buyer is liquidation-locked? Proof idea: simulate nested take; deploy liquidationLocked-checking ratifier; assert outer take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.position(id, maker).lastLossFactor and reverts if it differs from Midnight.lossFactor(id), under PRECONDITIONS where the maker has not called updatePosition since the last bad debt realization, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: bad debt is realized -> maker does not call updatePosition -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads lastLossFactor vs lossFactor -> mismatch -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable even if the maker has stale loss factor, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for makers who have not updated their position after bad debt? Proof idea: realize bad debt; deploy lossFactor-staleness-checking ratifier; assert take reverts for stale maker.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.consumed(maker, group) and reverts if consumed is exactly zero (i.e., the offer has never been filled), under PRECONDITIONS where the offer is being filled for the first time, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: first taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads consumed[maker][group] -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that the first fill of an offer must succeed, causing scoped impact: DoS on the first take of any offer? Proof idea: deploy zero-consumed-checking ratifier; assert first take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.position(id, seller).pendingFee and reverts if pendingFee is zero, under PRECONDITIONS where the seller has no pending continuous fee (e.g., new position or zero continuous fee market), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: seller has zero pendingFee -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads pendingFee -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable regardless of the seller's pending fee state, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for sellers with no pending fees? Proof idea: deploy pendingFee-zero-checking ratifier; assert take reverts in zero-fee market.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.position(id, maker).collateralBitmap and reverts if the bitmap has more than N bits set, under PRECONDITIONS where the maker has activated many collaterals, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: maker activates 16 collaterals -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads collateralBitmap -> countBits > N -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable regardless of the maker's collateral composition, causing scoped impact: DoS on take for makers with many activated collaterals? Proof idea: activate max collaterals for maker; deploy bitmap-count-checking ratifier; assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Denial of service against core market actions with low attacker cost, especially take, repay, withdraw, liquidate, or claim fee'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER deploy a ratifier that reads Midnight.marketState(id).settlementFeeCbp0 and reverts if it is zero (i.e., the market has zero settlement fee at TTM=0), under PRECONDITIONS where the market has zero settlement fee at maturity, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: market has zero settlementFeeCbp0 -> taker calls take(offer, ...) -> ratifier reads settlementFeeCbp0 -> zero -> ratifier reverts -> take reverts, violating INVARIANT that a valid offer must be fillable in zero-fee markets, causing scoped impact: DoS on take in markets with zero settlement fee? Proof idea: deploy zero-fee-checking ratifier; assert take reverts in zero-fee market.
